executiveSummary.summary: Strong educational background meets a real need, but you're entering a crowded market dominated by Khan Academy with limited resources. The Socratic dialogue approach is differentiated but unproven at scale. Your constraints (solo, $10K) require extreme focus - likely on one math topic and B2C initially. Viable but needs strategic narrowing.

marketSize.methodology: Based on 50M US students grades 6-12, ~15% use paid tutoring, ~10% willing to try AI-first solution

competitors.name: Khan Academy (Khanmigo)
competitors.description: Free platform with AI tutor powered by GPT-4
competitors.weakness: Generic experience, limited Socratic method implementation

competitors.name: Photomath
competitors.weakness: Doesn't teach process, gives answers directly

marketAnalysis.positioning: The only AI tutor that teaches like a human - through questions, not answers
marketAnalysis.timing: Post-COVID learning gaps create urgency, but AI fatigue in education is growing

valueProposition.coreProblem: Middle-class families can't afford $50-100/hr tutors but need more than Khan Academy videos for struggling students
valueProposition.targetCustomer: Parents of grades 6-12 students earning $50-150K household income, child struggling with math concepts
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: AI that asks questions instead of giving answers, forcing students to think through problems
valueProposition.whyNow: GPT-4 level models finally enable natural Socratic dialogue that wasn't possible 2 years ago

businessModel.revenueStrategy: Subscription SaaS with freemium trial (7 days free, then $29/mo)
businessModel.pricingFramework: $29/mo positioned between free Khan Academy and $200+/mo human tutoring

unitEconomics.estimatedCAC: $40-60 via content marketing and parent Facebook groups

businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: High marginal costs due to AI inference, but improves with scale and model efficiency gains

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: Next.js frontend, Node.js backend, OpenAI API, PostgreSQL, Stripe

buildVsBuy.rationale: OpenAI API cheaper than training custom model
Core differentiator requires custom prompt engineering

technicalRisks: OpenAI API costs scale with usage
Handling edge cases in math problems

risks.description: Khan Academy could improve Khanmigo's Socratic capabilities overnight
risks.mitigation: Focus on specific math topics they don't prioritize, build superior parent reporting

risks.description: Parent resistance to AI tutoring for children
risks.mitigation: Emphasize human oversight, transparent AI decision-making, focus on learning outcomes

risks.description: $10K insufficient for 6+ months of development and customer acquisition
risks.mitigation: Presell subscriptions, consider part-time development while freelancing

risks.description: AI inference costs could exceed revenue per user
risks.mitigation: Monitor unit economics closely, optimize prompts for efficiency

risks.description: Schools banning AI tools could limit adoption
risks.mitigation: Focus on after-school/homework help market initially

week1to4: Interview 20 parents of middle schoolers struggling with math
Test Socratic dialogue prompts with 5 students
week9to12: Measure actual learning outcomes vs engagement
Refine Socratic dialogue based on usage data

decisionAgenda.question: Should you focus on one math topic (Algebra 1) or cover grades 6-12 math?

options.tradeoff: Gain: faster MVP, better quality vs Loss: smaller addressable market
Gain: larger market vs Loss: 6+ month longer development, lower quality

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with Algebra 1 only. Your $10K constraint requires extreme focus. Algebra 1 is where most students first struggle with abstract math concepts - perfect for Socratic method.

decisionAgenda.question: Should you target parents (B2C) or schools (B2B)?

options.tradeoff: Gain: faster sales cycles, higher margins vs Loss: higher CAC, individual convincing
Gain: larger deals, built-in distribution vs Loss: 12+ month sales cycles, procurement complexity

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start B2C. Schools have 12+ month procurement cycles you can't afford. Parents buy tutoring immediately when their child struggles. Pivot to B2B later with proven outcomes.

decisionAgenda.question: How will you fund development beyond your $10K?

options.tradeoff: Gain: immediate validation and cash vs Loss: pressure to deliver quickly
Gain: steady income vs Loss: slower progress, divided attention
Gain: full focus vs Loss: dilution, time spent fundraising

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Presell subscriptions after beta validation. Offer 50% discount for annual prepay. This validates demand and extends runway without dilution.
